en: Now when they had passed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they came to Thessalonica, where there was a synagogue of the Jews.
zh: 保羅和西拉經過暗非波里、亞波羅尼亞，來到帖撒羅尼迦，在那裡有猶太人的會堂。

en: And Paul went in, as was his custom, and on three Sabbath days he reasoned with them from the Scriptures,
zh: 保羅照他的習慣進去，一連三個安息日，根據聖經與他們辯論，

en: explaining and proving that it was necessary for the Christ to suffer and to rise from the dead, and saying, "This Jesus, whom I proclaim to you, is the Christ."
zh: 講解證明基督必須受害，從死人中復活，說：“我所傳給你們的這位耶穌，就是基督。”

en: And some of them were persuaded and joined Paul and Silas, as did a great many of the devout Greeks and not a few of the leading women.
zh: 他們中間有人給說服了，就附從了保羅和西拉；還有一大群虔誠的希臘人，和不少顯要的婦女。

en: But the Jews were jealous, and taking some wicked men of the rabble, they formed a mob, set the city in an uproar, and attacked the house of Jason, seeking to bring them out to the crowd.
zh: 但猶太人嫉妒起來，招聚了一些市井流氓，糾合成群，騷動全城，衝進了耶孫的家，搜索保羅和西拉，要把他們交給民眾。

en: And when they could not find them, they dragged Jason and some of the brothers before the city authorities, shouting, "These men who have turned the world upside down have come here also,
zh: 搜索不到他們，就把耶孫和幾個弟兄，拉到地方長官那裡，喊叫著說：“這些擾亂天下的人，也到這裡來了，

en: and Jason has received them, and they are all acting against the decrees of Caesar, saying that there is another king, Jesus."
zh: 耶孫卻收留他們。這些人都違背凱撒的法令，說另外還有一個王耶穌。”

en: And the people and the city authorities were disturbed when they heard these things.
zh: 群眾和地方長官聽見這話，就驚慌起來，

en: And when they had taken money as security from Jason and the rest, they let them go.
zh: 取得耶孫和其餘的人簽保後，才放了他們。

en: The brothers immediately sent Paul and Silas away by night to Berea, and when they arrived they went into the Jewish synagogue.
zh: 弟兄們當夜立刻送保羅和西拉往比里亞去。二人到了，就進入猶太人的會堂。

en: Now these Jews were more noble than those in Thessalonica; they received the word with all eagerness, examining the Scriptures daily to see if these things were so.
zh: 這裡的人，比帖撒羅尼迦人開明，熱切接受主的道，天天考查聖經，要知道所聽的是否與聖經相符。

en: Many of them therefore believed, with not a few Greek women of high standing as well as men.
zh: 結果他們中間有很多人信了，也有高貴的希臘婦女，男的也不少。

en: But when the Jews from Thessalonica learned that the word of God was proclaimed by Paul at Berea also, they came there too, agitating and stirring up the crowds.
zh: 但帖撒羅尼迦的猶太人，知道保羅又在比里亞宣講 神的道，就到那裡去，煽動挑撥群眾。

en: Then the brothers immediately sent Paul off on his way to the sea, but Silas and Timothy remained there.
zh: 弟兄們立刻把保羅送到海邊去；西拉和提摩太仍然留在比里亞。

en: Those who conducted Paul brought him as far as Athens, and after receiving a command for Silas and Timothy to come to him as soon as possible, they departed.
zh: 護送保羅的人帶他到了雅典；保羅吩咐他們叫西拉和提摩太趕快去見他，於是他們就回去了。

en: Now while Paul was waiting for them at Athens, his spirit was provoked within him as he saw that the city was full of idols.
zh: 保羅在雅典等候他們的時候，看見滿城都是偶像，心靈十分忿激。

en: So he reasoned in the synagogue with the Jews and the devout persons, and in the marketplace every day with those who happened to be there.
zh: 於是在會堂裡，同猶太人和虔誠的人辯論；並且天天在市中心和所遇見的人辯論。

en: Some of the Epicurean and Stoic philosophers also conversed with him. And some said, "What does this babbler wish to say?" Others said, "He seems to be a preacher of foreign divinities"- because he was preaching Jesus and the resurrection.
zh: 還有一些伊壁鳩魯派和斯多亞派的哲學家也同他爭論，有的說：“這個拾人牙慧的人要說甚麼呢？”有的說：“他似乎是一個宣傳外地鬼神的人。”這是因為保羅傳揚耶穌和復活的道理。

en: And they took hold of him and brought him to the Areopagus, saying, "May we know what this new teaching is that you are presenting?
zh: 他們拉著保羅，把他帶到亞略．巴古那裡，說：“你所講的這個新道理，我們可以知道嗎？

en: For you bring some strange things to our ears. We wish to know therefore what these things mean."
zh: 因為你把一切新奇的事，傳到我們耳中，我們願意知道這些事是甚麼意思。”

en: Now all the Athenians and the foreigners who lived there would spend their time in nothing except telling or hearing something new.
zh: 原來所有雅典人和外僑，專好談論和打聽新奇的事，來打發時間。

en: So Paul, standing in the midst of the Areopagus, said: "Men of Athens, I perceive that in every way you are very religious.
zh: 保羅站在亞略．巴古當中，說：“各位雅典人，我看你們在各方面都非常敬畏鬼神。

en: For as I passed along and observed the objects of your worship, I found also an altar with this inscription, 'To the unknown god.' What therefore you worship as unknown, this I proclaim to you.
zh: 我走路的時候，仔細看你們所敬拜的，發現有一座壇，上面寫著‘獻給不認識的神’。我現在把你們不認識而敬拜的這位神，傳給你們。

en: The God who made the world and everything in it, being Lord of heaven and earth, does not live in temples made by man,
zh: 創造宇宙和其中萬有的 神，既然是天地的主，就不住在人手所造的殿宇，

en: nor is he served by human hands, as though he needed anything, since he himself gives to all mankind life and breath and everything.
zh: 也不受人手的服事，好像他缺少甚麼；他自己反而把生命、氣息和一切，賜給萬人。

en: And he made from one man every nation of mankind to live on all the face of the earth, having determined allotted periods and the boundaries of their dwelling place,
zh: 他從一個本源造出了萬族來，使他們住在整個大地上，並且定了他們的期限和居住的疆界，

en: that they should seek God, in the hope that they might feel their way toward him and find him. Yet he is actually not far from each one of us,
zh: 要他們尋求 神，或者可以摸索而找到他。其實他離我們各人不遠，

en: for "'In him we live and move and have our being'; as even some of your own poets have said, "' For we are indeed his offspring.'
zh: 因著他我們可以生存、活動、存在，就如你們有些詩人說：‘原來我們也是他的子孫。’

en: Being then God's offspring, we ought not to think that the divine being is like gold or silver or stone, an image formed by the art and imagination of man.
zh: 我們既然是 神的子孫，就不應該以為他的神性是好像人用手藝、心思所雕刻的金銀石頭一樣。

en: The times of ignorance God overlooked, but now he commands all people everywhere to repent,
zh: 過去那無知的時代， 神不加以追究；現在，他卻吩咐各處的人都要悔改，

en: because he has fixed a day on which he will judge the world in righteousness by a man whom he has appointed; and of this he has given assurance to all by raising him from the dead."
zh: 因為他已經定好了日子，要藉著他所立的人，按公義審判天下，並且使他從死人中復活，給萬人作一個可信的憑據。”

en: Now when they heard of the resurrection of the dead, some mocked. But others said, "We will hear you again about this."
zh: 眾人一聽到死人復活的事，就譏笑他，但有的說：“我們要再聽聽你講這件事！”

en: So Paul went out from their midst.
zh: 這樣，保羅就離開他們。

en: But some men joined him and believed, among them Dionysius the Areopagite and a woman named Damaris and others with them.
zh: 但有幾個人接近他，並且信了主，其中有亞略．巴古的議員丟尼修，一個名叫戴馬里的女子，還有其他在一起的人。
